description: Fix code review issues: reference equality for event identity, drag slot ID parsing fragility, heuristic suggestion deduplication, and AudioContext leak
steps: In calendar/page.tsx, replace `e === selectedEvent` and `e === draggedEvent` with ID-based comparison using `e.id === selectedEvent.id` (with fallback to reference for local events without IDs).
Fix drag slot ID parsing to use a non-ambiguous delimiter or parse knowing exact date segment positions.
In AIContextProvider.tsx, add a Set or Map with cooldown tracking to prevent duplicate suggestion toasts.
In FocusMode.tsx, capture ambient audio nodes in a local variable inside the effect and use that in the cleanup closure instead of reading from the ref.
acceptance_criteria: Event edits and drag-reschedule use ID-based comparison, not reference equality
Drag slot ID parsing correctly handles the date format without ambiguity
Identical AI suggestions are suppressed within a 5-minute cooldown window
AudioContext cleanup uses closure-captured nodes, not ref
npm run build passes without errors
findings: All 4 review issues fixed in a single commit. Build passes cleanly. Slot ID delimiter changed from hyphen to colon (slot:YYYY-MM-DD:HH). Event identity uses e.id comparison with fallback to reference equality for locally-created events without IDs. Deduplication uses a Map with 5-minute TTL keyed on action type + entity ID. AudioContext cleanup now captures the nodes variable in the closure rather than reading from the mutable ref.
